title: They’ve Adjusted
pressure: Assistants are no longer reacting to inconsistency. They are adjusting to it. They anticipate which doctor gets flexibility and change their behavior accordingly. No one talks about fairness anymore. The system is now predictable — but not fair.

A.escalation_text: Adaptation stabilizes the system. But fairness disappears completely.

second_choices.label: Fully accept
second_choices.cost: Fairness disappears into routine while the team keeps optimizing around who gets flexibility.

second_choices.label: Question the adaptation
second_choices.cost: You disturb a stable unfair equilibrium and may be seen as difficult or naive.

action_decision.prompt: What is lost when adaptation becomes normal?

B.escalation_text: Once you see it as distortion, you realize this is not alignment — it is survival behavior.

second_choices.label: Keep it internal
second_choices.cost: You carry accurate pattern recognition alone while the office keeps adapting without shared accountability.

second_choices.label: Define the pattern clearly
second_choices.cost: You concentrate pressure by naming what people survived by not naming out loud.

action_decision.prompt: If this is distortion, what defines correct behavior?

second_choices.label: Stay efficiency-focused
second_choices.cost: Throughput stays high while ethical drift stays off the agenda.

second_choices.label: Reintroduce principle
second_choices.cost: You slow decisions by forcing purpose and fairness back into the same conversation.

D.escalation_text: Neutral observation slowly becomes passive agreement.

second_choices.label: Remain neutral
second_choices.cost: Neutrality without a stance becomes silent agreement with the adapted unfair baseline.

second_choices.label: Take a position internally
second_choices.cost: You accept ongoing inner tension and can no longer pretend you have no standard.
